11: fn cleanup_candidate_branches(
12:     trunk: &str,
13:     managed_branches: &[String],
14:     local_branches: &[String],
15: ) -> Vec<String> {
16:     let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();
17:     let mut branches = Vec::new();
18: 
19:     for branch in managed_branches {
20:         if branch != trunk && seen.insert(branch.clone()) {
21:             branches.push(branch.clone());
22:         }
23:     }
24: 
25:     for branch in local_branches {
26:         if branch != trunk && seen.insert(branch.clone()) {
27:             branches.push(branch.clone());
28:         }
29:     }
30: 
31:     branches
32: }
33: 
34: fn cleanup_reason(
35:     pr_info: Option<&github::PrInfo>,
36:     merged_via_git: bool,
37:     merged_via_diff: bool,
38: ) -> Option<&'static str> {
39:     if let Some(pr) = pr_info {
40:         if pr.merged {
41:             Some("merged")
42:         } else if pr.state == "CLOSED" {
43:             Some("pr_closed")
44:         } else {
45:             None
46:         }
47:     } else if merged_via_git || merged_via_diff {
48:         Some("merged")
49:     } else {
50:         None
51:     }
52: }
53: 
54: fn inside_worktree_path(current_dir: &str, worktree_path: &str) -> bool {
55:     fn normalize(path: &str) -> std::path::PathBuf {
56:         std::fs::canonicalize(path).unwrap_or_else(|_| std::path::PathBuf::from(path))
57:     }
58: 
59:     let current = normalize(current_dir);
60:     let worktree = normalize(worktree_path);
61:     current == worktree || current.starts_with(&worktree)
62: }
63: 
64: pub fn run(dry_run: bool, autostash: bool, force: bool) -> Result<()> {
65:     let state = StackState::load()?;
66:     if let Some(root) = git::current_linked_worktree_root()? {
67:         ui::linked_worktree_warning(&root);
68:     }
69: 
70:     if dry_run {
71:         ui::header("Sync preview (--dry-run, no changes will be made)");
72:         ui::info(&format!("Would fetch from `{}`", state.remote));
73:         ui::info(&format!(
74:             "Would update `{}` to latest remote (no checkout needed)",
75:             state.trunk
76:         ));
77: 
78:         let dry_worktree_map: std::collections::HashMap<String, String> = git::worktree_list()
79:             .unwrap_or_default()
80:             .into_iter()
81:             .filter(|wt| wt.path.contains("/.worktrees/"))
82:             .filter_map(|wt| wt.branch.map(|b| (b, wt.path)))
83:             .collect();
84: 
85:         let managed_branches: Vec<String> = state.branches.keys().cloned().collect();
86:         for branch_name in &managed_branches {
87:             let meta = state.get_branch(branch_name)?;
88:             if meta.pr_number.is_some() {
89:                 ui::info(&format!(
90:                     "Would check if PR for `{branch_name}` is merged or closed"
91:                 ));
92:                 if let Some(wt_path) = dry_worktree_map.get(branch_name.as_str()) {
93:                     ui::info(&format!("  → Would remove worktree at `{wt_path}`"));
94:                 }
95:             }
96:         }
97: 
98:         let order = state.topo_order();
99:         let mut any_restack = false;
100:         for branch_name in &order {
101:             let meta = state.get_branch(branch_name)?;
102:             let parent = &meta.parent;
103:             let stored_head = &meta.parent_head;
104:             if let Ok(current_tip) = git::rev_parse(parent) {
105:                 if current_tip != *stored_head {
106:                     ui::info(&format!("Would restack `{branch_name}` onto `{parent}`"));
107:                     any_restack = true;
108:                 }
109:             }
110:         }
111: 
112:         if !any_restack {
113:             ui::info("No restacking needed based on current local state");
114:         }
115: 
116:         ui::hint("Run `ez sync` (without --dry-run) to apply these changes");
117:         return Ok(());
118:     }
119: 
120:     // Autostash: stash before any mutations.
121:     let stashed = if autostash {
122:         let did_stash = git::stash_push()?;
123:         if did_stash {
124:             ui::info("Stashed uncommitted changes (--autostash)");
125:         }
126:         did_stash
127:     } else {
128:         false
129:     };
130: 
131:     let result = run_sync_inner(force);
132: 
133:     if stashed {
134:         if let Err(e) = git::stash_pop() {
135:             ui::warn(&format!("Failed to pop autostash: {e}"));
136:         } else {
137:             ui::info("Restored stashed changes");
138:         }
139:     }
140: 
141:     result
142: }

144: fn run_sync_inner(force: bool) -> Result<()> {
145:     let mut state = StackState::load()?;
146:     let original_branch = git::current_branch()?;
147:     let original_root = git::repo_root()?;
148:     let mut shell_cd_path: Option<String> = None;
149:     let mut cleaned_current_worktree = false;
150: 
151:     // Fetch from remote.
152:     ui::info(&format!("Fetching from `{}`...", state.remote));
153:     git::fetch(&state.remote)?;
154: 
155:     match git::reset_branch_to_latest_remote(
156:         &state.remote,
157:         &state.trunk,
158:         &original_branch,
159:         &original_root,
160:     ) {
161:         Ok(true) => ui::info(&format!(
162:             "Reset `{}` to latest `{}/{}`",
163:             state.trunk, state.remote, state.trunk
164:         )),
165:         Ok(false) => {}
166:         Err(e) => ui::warn(&format!("Could not update `{}` — {e}", state.trunk)),
167:     }
168: 
169:     // Build branch→worktree map for pruning merged branches.
170:     // Only include worktrees under .worktrees/ — the main worktree must never be removed.
171:     let worktree_map: std::collections::HashMap<String, String> = git::worktree_list()
172:         .unwrap_or_default()
173:         .into_iter()
174:         .filter(|wt| wt.path.contains("/.worktrees/"))
175:         .filter_map(|wt| wt.branch.map(|b| (b, wt.path)))
176:         .collect();
177:     let main_root = git::main_worktree_root().unwrap_or_else(|_| original_root.clone());
178:     let current_dir = std::env::current_dir()
179:         .ok()
180:         .map(|path| path.to_string_lossy().into_owned())
181:         .unwrap_or_default();
182: 
183:     // Detect merged PRs and clean up.
184:     let managed_branches = {
185:         let mut order = state.topo_order();
186:         // Also include branches not in topo order (orphaned branches).
187:         for key in state.branches.keys() {
188:             if !order.contains(key) {
189:                 order.push(key.clone());
190:             }
191:         }
192:         order
193:     };
194:     let local_branches = git::branch_list().unwrap_or_default();
195:     let cleanup_candidates =
196:         cleanup_candidate_branches(&state.trunk, &managed_branches, &local_branches);
197:     let mut cleaned = Vec::new();
198:     let has_any_prs = !cleanup_candidates.is_empty();
199:     let pr_statuses = if has_any_prs {
200:         let sp = ui::spinner("Checking PR states...");
201:         let bulk_repo = state.repo.clone().filter(|s| !s.is_empty());
202:         let statuses = github::get_all_pr_statuses_in_repo(bulk_repo.as_deref());
203:         sp.finish_and_clear();
204:         statuses
205:     } else {
206:         std::collections::HashMap::new()
207:     };
208: 
209:     for branch_name in &cleanup_candidates {
210:         let meta = state.get_branch(branch_name).ok().cloned();
211:         let is_managed = meta.is_some();
212:         let pr_info = pr_statuses.get(branch_name.as_str());
213:         let pr_number = meta
214:             .as_ref()
215:             .and_then(|m| m.pr_number)
216:             .or(pr_info.map(|pr| pr.number));
217:         let parent = meta.as_ref().map(|m| m.parent.clone());
218: 
219:         // Auto-clean branches that no longer exist locally (deleted outside of ez).
220:         if !git::branch_exists(branch_name) {
221:             if !is_managed {
222:                 continue;
223:             }
224:             let parent = parent.clone().expect("managed branch should have a parent");
225:             if git::branch_exists(&parent) {
226:                 let _ = state.reparent_children_preserving_parent_head(branch_name, &parent)?;
227:             } else {
228:                 // Parent also deleted — reparent children to trunk, but keep their old base SHA
229:                 // so a later restack still knows what to rebase from.
230:                 let trunk_name = state.trunk.clone();
231:                 let _ = state.reparent_children_preserving_parent_head(branch_name, &trunk_name)?;
232:             }
233:             state.remove_branch(branch_name);
234:             ui::info(&format!("Cleaned up `{branch_name}` (deleted outside ez)"));
235:             cleaned.push(branch_name.clone());
236:             continue;
237:         }
238: 
239:         let merged_via_git = if pr_info.is_none() {
240:             git::is_ancestor(branch_name, &state.trunk)
241:         } else {
242:             false
243:         };
244: 
245:         // Diff-level check: only for branches WITHOUT a PR.
246:         // If a PR exists, the PR status is authoritative. An empty diff might just
247:         // mean someone cherry-picked the changes, not that the PR was merged.
248:         let merged_via_diff =
249:             if is_managed && pr_info.is_none() && !merged_via_git && pr_number.is_none() {
250:                 let range = format!("{}...{}", state.trunk, branch_name);
251:                 git::diff(&range, true, false)
252:                     .map(|stat| stat.trim().is_empty())
253:                     .unwrap_or(false)
254:             } else {
255:                 false
256:             };
257: 
258:         let cleanup_reason = cleanup_reason(pr_info, merged_via_git, merged_via_diff);
259: 
260:         if cleanup_reason.is_none() {
261:             continue;
262:         }
263: 
264:         let cleanup_reason = cleanup_reason.unwrap_or("merged");
265: 
266:         // Remove worktree for this branch (if any) before mutating stack state.
267:         // If cleanup fails, keep the branch tracked so `ez sync --force` or `ez delete`
268:         // can recover it later.
269:         if let Some(wt_path) = worktree_map.get(branch_name.as_str()) {
270:             let is_current_worktree = inside_worktree_path(&current_dir, wt_path)
271:                 || inside_worktree_path(&original_root, wt_path);
272:             if is_current_worktree && let Err(e) = std::env::set_current_dir(&main_root) {
273:                 ui::warn(&format!(
274:                     "Could not move out of worktree `{wt_path}` before cleanup: {e}"
275:                 ));
276:                 ui::info(&format!(
277:                     "Kept `{branch_name}` tracked because cleanup did not complete"
278:                 ));
279:                 ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
280:                     "cmd": "sync",
281:                     "branch": branch_name,
282:                     "action": "cleanup_skipped",
283:                     "reason": "cwd_move_failed",
284:                     "parent": parent,
285:                     "worktree": wt_path,
286:                 }));
287:                 continue;
288:             }
289:             let result = if force {
290:                 git::worktree_remove_force(wt_path)
291:             } else {
292:                 git::worktree_remove(wt_path)
293:             };
294:             match result {
295:                 Ok(()) => {
296:                     ui::info(&format!("Removed worktree at `{wt_path}`"));
297:                     if is_current_worktree {
298:                         shell_cd_path = Some(main_root.clone());
299:                         cleaned_current_worktree = true;
300:                     }
301:                 }
302:                 Err(e) => {
303:                     ui::warn(&format!(
304:                         "Could not remove worktree at `{wt_path}`: {e}\n  Hint: use `ez sync --force` to discard uncommitted changes"
305:                     ));
306:                     ui::info(&format!(
307:                         "Kept `{branch_name}` tracked because cleanup did not complete"
308:                     ));
309:                     ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
310:                         "cmd": "sync",
311:                         "branch": branch_name,
312:                         "action": "cleanup_skipped",
313:                         "reason": "worktree_remove_failed",
314:                         "parent": parent,
315:                         "worktree": wt_path,
316:                     }));
317:                     continue;
318:                 }
319:             }
320:         }
321: 
322:         // If we're on the branch being deleted, switch to trunk first.
323:         if *branch_name == original_branch && !cleaned_current_worktree {
324:             if let Err(e) = git::checkout(&state.trunk) {
325:                 ui::warn(&format!("Could not switch to trunk: {e}"));
326:                 ui::info(&format!(
327:                     "Kept `{branch_name}` tracked because cleanup did not complete"
328:                 ));
329:                 ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
330:                     "cmd": "sync",
331:                     "branch": branch_name,
332:                     "action": "cleanup_skipped",
333:                     "reason": "checkout_failed",
334:                     "parent": parent,
335:                 }));
336:                 continue;
337:             }
338:         }
339: 
340:         // Delete local branch. If this fails, keep the branch tracked so cleanup can be retried.
341:         if git::branch_exists(branch_name)
342:             && let Err(e) = git::delete_branch(branch_name, true)
343:         {
344:             ui::warn(&format!(
345:                 "Could not delete local branch `{branch_name}`: {e}"
346:             ));
347:             ui::info(&format!(
348:                 "Kept `{branch_name}` tracked because cleanup did not complete"
349:             ));
350:             ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
351:                 "cmd": "sync",
352:                 "branch": branch_name,
353:                 "action": "cleanup_skipped",
354:                 "reason": "branch_delete_failed",
355:                 "parent": parent,
356:             }));
357:             continue;
358:         }
359: 
360:         if is_managed {
361:             let parent_name = parent.clone().expect("managed branch should have a parent");
362:             let _ = state.reparent_children_preserving_parent_head(branch_name, &parent_name)?;
363: 
364:             state.remove_branch(branch_name);
365:         }
366: 
367:         let cleanup_label = if cleanup_reason == "pr_closed" {
368:             "PR closed"
369:         } else {
370:             "merged"
371:         };
372:         ui::info(&format!("Cleaned up `{branch_name}` ({cleanup_label})"));
373:         ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
374:             "cmd": "sync",
375:             "branch": branch_name,
376:             "action": "cleaned",
377:             "reason": cleanup_reason,
378:         }));
379:         cleaned.push(branch_name.clone());
380:     }
381: 
382:     // Pre-flight checks: detect merge commits, redundant branches, stale metadata.
383:     let checks = preflight::check_all(&state);
384:     if !checks.is_empty() {
385:         ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
386:             "cmd": "sync",
387:             "action": "preflight",
388:             "summary": preflight::summary(&checks),
389:         }));
390:     }
391:     if preflight::report_and_check(&checks, force) {
392:         bail!(EzError::UserMessage(
393:             "sync aborted — resolve the issues above or use `ez sync --force`".to_string()
394:         ));
395:     }
396: 
397:     // Build a set of fully-redundant branches so we can skip their rebase.
398:     let redundant_branches: std::collections::HashSet<String> = checks
399:         .iter()
400:         .filter(|c| c.all_redundant)
401:         .map(|c| c.branch.clone())
402:         .collect();
403: 
404:     // Restack remaining branches.
405:     let order = state.topo_order();
406:     let mut restacked = 0;
407: 
408:     for branch_name in &order {
409:         let meta = state.get_branch(branch_name)?;
410:         let parent = meta.parent.clone();
411:         let stored_parent_head = meta.parent_head.clone();
412: 
413:         // Skip branches that no longer exist (shouldn't happen after cleanup above, but be safe).
414:         if !git::branch_exists(branch_name) {
415:             continue;
416:         }
417: 
418:         let current_parent_tip = git::rev_parse(&parent)?;
419: 
420:         if current_parent_tip == stored_parent_head {
421:             continue;
422:         }
423: 
424:         // Instead of skipping, detach worktree HEAD so rebase can proceed.
425:         let worktree_path = if let Ok(Some(wt_path)) = git::branch_checked_out_elsewhere(branch_name, &original_root) {
426:             ui::info(&format!("Detaching `{branch_name}` in worktree `{wt_path}` for rebase..."));
427:             git::detach_worktree_head(&wt_path)?;
428:             Some(wt_path)
429:         } else {
430:             None
431:         };
432: 
433:         // If all commits are redundant, skip rebase and just update metadata.
434:         if redundant_branches.contains(branch_name) {
435:             let meta = state.get_branch_mut(branch_name)?;
436:             meta.parent_head = current_parent_tip;
437:             ui::info(&format!(
438:                 "Skipped rebase for `{branch_name}` — all commits already in `{parent}` (metadata updated)"
439:             ));
440:             ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
441:                 "cmd": "sync",
442:                 "branch": branch_name,
443:                 "action": "redundant_skip",
444:                 "parent": parent,
445:             }));
446:             // Reattach worktree if we detached it.
447:             if let Some(ref wt_path) = worktree_path {
448:                 let _ = git::reattach_worktree(wt_path, branch_name);
449:             }
450:             restacked += 1;
451:             continue;
452:         }
453: 
454:         let before_sha = git::rev_parse(branch_name).unwrap_or_default();
455: 
456:         // If merge commits were detected but --force was used, use plain rebase
457:         // (better patch-id skipping) instead of rebase --onto.
458:         let has_merges = checks.iter().any(|c| c.branch == *branch_name && c.merge_commits > 0);
459: 
460:         let sp = ui::spinner(&format!("Restacking `{branch_name}` onto `{parent}`..."));
461:         let outcome = if has_merges {
462:             ui::info(&format!(
463:                 "Branch `{branch_name}` has merge commits — using safe rebase mode"
464:             ));
465:             match git::rebase(&parent, branch_name) {
466:                 Ok(true) => git::RebaseOutcome::RebasingComplete,
467:                 Ok(false) => git::RebaseOutcome::Conflict(git::RebaseConflict {
468:                     conflicting_files: vec![],
469:                     stderr: "rebase conflict during safe rebase mode".to_string(),
470:                 }),
471:                 Err(e) => return Err(e),
472:             }
473:         } else {
474:             git::rebase_onto(&current_parent_tip, &stored_parent_head, branch_name)?
475:         };
476:         sp.finish_and_clear();
477: 
478:         match outcome {
479:             git::RebaseOutcome::RebasingComplete => {
480:                 let meta = state.get_branch_mut(branch_name)?;
481:                 meta.parent_head = current_parent_tip;
482:                 restacked += 1;
483:                 ui::info(&format!("Restacked `{branch_name}` onto `{parent}`"));
484: 
485:                 // Post-restack: detect and auto-drop redundant commits.
486:                 let mut redundant_count: u64 = 0;
487:                 if let Ok(cherry) = git::cherry(&parent, branch_name) {
488:                     let redundant: Vec<&str> =
489:                         cherry.lines().filter(|l| l.starts_with("- ")).collect();
490:                     if !redundant.is_empty() {
491:                         redundant_count = redundant.len() as u64;
492:                         ui::info(&format!(
493:                             "Dropping {redundant_count} redundant commit(s) from `{branch_name}` (already in `{parent}`)",
494:                         ));
495:                         match git::rebase(&parent, branch_name) {
496:                             Ok(true) => {
497:                                 ui::info(&format!(
498:                                     "Dropped redundant commits from `{branch_name}`"
499:                                 ));
500:                             }
501:                             Ok(false) => {
502:                                 ui::warn(&format!(
503:                                     "Could not auto-drop redundant commits from `{branch_name}` (conflict)"
504:                                 ));
505:                                 ui::hint(&format!(
506:                                     "Run `git rebase {parent}` on `{branch_name}` manually and skip redundant commits"
507:                                 ));
508:                             }
509:                             Err(e) => {
510:                                 ui::warn(&format!(
511:                                     "Could not clean up redundant commits from `{branch_name}`: {e}"
512:                                 ));
513:                             }
514:                         }
515:                     }
516:                 }
517: 
518:                 let after_sha = git::rev_parse(branch_name).unwrap_or_default();
519:                 ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
520:                     "cmd": "sync",
521:                     "branch": branch_name,
522:                     "action": "restacked",
523:                     "parent": parent,
524:                     "before": &before_sha[..before_sha.len().min(7)],
525:                     "after": &after_sha[..after_sha.len().min(7)],
526:                     "redundant_commits": redundant_count,
527:                     "safe_rebase_mode": has_merges,
528:                 }));
529: 
530:                 // Reattach worktree if we detached it.
531:                 if let Some(ref wt_path) = worktree_path {
532:                     if !git::reattach_worktree(wt_path, branch_name)? {
533:                         ui::warn(&format!(
534:                             "Could not reattach `{branch_name}` in worktree `{wt_path}` — \
535:                              worktree may have dirty files that conflict with rebased commits.\n  \
536:                              Run `cd {wt_path} && git checkout {branch_name}` to reattach manually."
537:                         ));
538:                     }
539:                 }
540:             }
541:             git::RebaseOutcome::Conflict(conflict) => {
542:                 if let Some(ref wt_path) = worktree_path {
543:                     let _ = git::reattach_worktree(wt_path, branch_name);
544:                 }
545:                 // Save progress so the user can fix and continue.
546:                 state.save()?;
547:                 rebase_conflict::report("sync", branch_name, &parent, &conflict, "ez restack");
548:                 bail!(EzError::RebaseConflict(branch_name.clone()));
549:             }
550:         }
551:     }
552: 
553:     state.save()?;
554: 
555:     // Return to original branch if it still exists.
556:     // If it was cleaned up (merged), fall back to trunk — but trunk might be in another worktree.
557:     if cleaned_current_worktree {
558:         ui::info(&format!(
559:             "Current worktree `{original_branch}` was cleaned up — switched context to repo root"
560:         ));
561:     } else if git::branch_exists(&original_branch) {
562:         let _ = git::checkout(&original_branch);
563:     } else {
564:         match git::checkout(&state.trunk) {
565:             Ok(()) => ui::info(&format!(
566:                 "Previous branch `{original_branch}` was cleaned up — switched to `{}`",
567:                 state.trunk
568:             )),
569:             Err(_) => ui::warn(&format!(
570:                 "Previous branch `{original_branch}` was cleaned up. Switch to another branch manually \
571:                  (trunk may be checked out in another worktree)."
572:             )),
573:         }
574:     }
575: 
576:     if cleaned.is_empty() && restacked == 0 {
577:         ui::info("Everything is up to date");
578:     } else {
579:         ui::success(&format!(
580:             "Synced ({} cleaned, {} restacked)",
581:             cleaned.len(),
582:             restacked
583:         ));
584:     }
585: 
586:     // Prune stale worktree admin entries.
587:     let _ = git::worktree_prune();
588: 
589:     if let Some(path) = shell_cd_path {
590:         println!("{path}");
591:     }
592: 
593:     Ok(())
594: }

596: #[cfg(test)]
597: mod tests {
598:     use super::*;
599: 
600:     #[test]
601:     fn run_signatures_compile() {

627:     #[test]
628:     fn cleanup_reason_prefers_pr_state() {
629:         let closed = github::PrInfo {
630:             number: 42,
631:             url: String::new(),
632:             state: "CLOSED".to_string(),
633:             title: String::new(),
634:             base: "main".to_string(),
635:             is_draft: false,
636:             merged: false,
637:         };
638:         let merged = github::PrInfo {
639:             merged: true,
640:             ..closed.clone()
641:         };
642: 
643:         assert_eq!(cleanup_reason(Some(&merged), false, false), Some("merged"));
644:         assert_eq!(
645:             cleanup_reason(Some(&closed), false, false),
646:             Some("pr_closed")
647:         );
648:         assert_eq!(
649:             cleanup_reason(
650:                 Some(&github::PrInfo {
651:                     state: "OPEN".to_string(),
652:                     ..closed
653:                 }),
654:                 true,
655:                 true
656:             ),
657:             None
658:         );
659:         assert_eq!(cleanup_reason(None, true, false), Some("merged"));
660:         assert_eq!(cleanup_reason(None, false, true), Some("merged"));
661:         assert_eq!(cleanup_reason(None, false, false), None);
662:     }
